rationale: The UN projections indicate that the world population is expected to grow from 7.35 billion in 2015 to 8.1 billion in 2025, representing a growth of between 75 and 83 million people per year. The Senate has passed a reconciliation bill using a 'current policy' baseline, which extends the 2017 TCJA individual tax cuts. President Trump signed the bill on July 4, 2025. The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) signed into law by President Trump on July 4, 2025, modifies the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) to make domestic content requirements mandatory for obtaining the IRA's Production Tax Credits (45Y). The OBBBA harmonizes domestic content requirements under Section 48E with those under Section 45Y for projects beginning construction on or after June 16, 2025. Specifically, it introduces accelerated repeal schedules, shortens eligibility deadlines, tightens domestic content rules, and imposes new foreign entity of concern (FEOC) restrictions. Therefore, it is likely that domestic content requirements will become mandatory in 2025.

The domestic content requirements for obtaining the IRA's Investment Tax Credits (48Ε) have been made mandatory. President Trump signed H.R.1, the 'One Big Beautiful Bill Act,' on July 4, 2025, which amends several Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) tax credits and imposes specific domestic content percentages for Section 48E projects. These changes indicate that domestic-content compliance will become mandatory for full Investment Tax Credit eligibility in 2025, although some uncertainty remains regarding implementation details.

The House of Representatives passed a reconciliation bill (H.R. 1) on May 22, 2025, which amends several Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) tax credits, including the Advanced Manufacturing Production Credit (Section 45X). The bill introduces new Foreign Entity of Concern (FEOC) restrictions that would require domestic materials or subcomponents for certain tax credits, effectively mandating their use for eligibility. This change is likely to impact the Advanced Manufacturing Production Tax Credits (45Χ) starting in 2025.
